name: Port Phillip Estate Restaurant
address: 263 Red Hill Rd, Red Hill South VIC 3937
signature: The dining room inside the dramatic rammed-earth Port Phillip Estate building  -  confident, local-produce cooking under one of the Peninsula's most photographed ceilings
editorNote:
Port Phillip Estate's restaurant is the dining room that justifies the architecture  -  and the architecture is extraordinary. The 120-metre curved rammed-earth wall sweeps out over the hillside, the cellar door and dining room are tucked beneath it, and the view from the main room reaches across the vines to Port Phillip Bay on a clear day.

The cooking is hatted, confident, and built around Peninsula produce with enough polish to carry the room. The wine list leans into the estate's Port Phillip and Kooyong labels  -  both among the most serious single-vineyard programs in the country  -  with a thoughtful reach beyond the estate when appropriate.

Book for lunch on a sunny afternoon. Take the architecture seriously  -  it is a genuinely significant building  -  and pair one of the single-block Chardonnays with whatever the kitchen is running that day. This is the Peninsula at its most cinematic.

heroImage.alt: Restaurant dining imagery evocative of Port Phillip Estate Restaurant, Mornington Peninsula
